title: FAQ 10 - Who to Turn to for CRA Payroll Problems
full_text: Who should I turn to for help? Here's what ChatGPT says. Accountants versus tax lawyer versus corporate debt specialist. Okay. Accountants, never. Accountants are not lawyers. Accountants are cowards. Accountants will always say stupid crap like, well, I'm not going to risk my livelihood for you. Coward. Never, never, never do business with a coward. They'll turn on you in a second. accountants are the ones that say when the CRA is calling I'm not going to lose my license so you better cooperate with the audit they'll say this to you don't go to accountants for any information about the CRA then there's tax lawyers tax lawyers work for the crown they swore an oath to uphold the crown not justice so if they are commanded by the law society that they work for or are licensed by to screw you over, they will. Can't go to them. And corporate debt specialists, what the hell is that? What is that? I mean, I'm one, but I'm the only one with balls. So if you were to call accountants, tax lawyers, or debt specialists, ask them, are you working for me? Will you do whatever it takes to win? Will you cooperate with auditors or will you trespass them? Will you get me all my money back? Will you make it so I don't ever have to pay tax again? They'll probably say no. Right? No. So don't do business with someone that you're going to pay and have them not represent you, which is exactly what's been going on lately with lawyers. It's not a good scene at all.
